# @ledewire/browser

> LedeWire Browser SDK — buyer authentication, checkout, wallet, and purchases for web apps.
> Works as a CDN script tag (no build step) or as an ESM npm package.
> npm install @ledewire/browser
> Source: https://github.com/ledewire/ledewire-js-sdk
> API docs: https://ledewire.github.io/ledewire-js-sdk/api/

## CDN (no install required)

```html
<!-- Pin to a major version in production -->
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@ledewire/browser@0/dist/ledewire.min.js"></script>
<script>
  const lw = Ledewire.init({ apiKey: 'your_api_key' })
</script>
```

The global is `Ledewire`. All methods return Promises — use async/await or `.then()`.

## npm / ESM install

```bash
npm install @ledewire/browser
```

```ts
import { init, localStorageAdapter } from '@ledewire/browser'
```

## init() configuration

```ts
import { init } from '@ledewire/browser'
import type { BrowserClientConfig } from '@ledewire/browser'

const lw = init({
  apiKey: string,           // required — your LedeWire platform API key

  baseUrl?: string,         // defaults to 'https://api.ledewire.com'

  // Token persistence — defaults to MemoryTokenStorage (cleared on page unload)
  storage?: TokenStorage,

  onTokenRefreshed?: (tokens: StoredTokens) => void,
  onAuthExpired?: () => void,  // called when refresh fails — show login UI
})
```

Token refresh is automatic — never call a refresh method manually.

## localStorageAdapter

Persist sessions across page reloads and browser restarts using `localStorage`:

```ts
import { init, localStorageAdapter } from '@ledewire/browser'

const lw = init({
  apiKey: 'your_api_key',
  storage: localStorageAdapter(),     // persists under 'lw_tokens' key
  onAuthExpired: () => showLoginModal(),
})
```

## sessionStorageAdapter

Persist sessions within the current tab only (cleared on tab close) using `sessionStorage`.
Use this to prevent token leakage across tabs — recommended for embedded checkout widgets
and kiosk-mode UIs:

```ts
import { init, sessionStorageAdapter } from '@ledewire/browser'

const lw = init({
  apiKey: 'your_api_key',
  storage: sessionStorageAdapter(),
})
```

Use `MemoryTokenStorage` (default) for highest security — tokens are never written
to disk and are cleared when the page unloads.

## Field naming convention

All API response types use `snake_case` field names (`access_token`, `price_cents`,
`content_uri`), matching the wire format exactly.

SDK-owned types use `camelCase` where they do not map 1:1 to a raw API response
field: `StoredTokens.accessToken`, `StoredTokens.expiresAt: number` (a converted
Unix ms timestamp, not the raw `expires_at: string`).

Do not attempt to remap snake_case fields — the SDK has no `transformKeys` option.

## Complete method reference

### lw.config

```ts
lw.config.getPublic() → Promise<PublicConfigResponse>
// { google_client_id: string, ... }
// Safe to call before authentication — use to get the Google Client ID for sign-in UI.
```

### lw.auth (buyer auth)

```ts
lw.auth.signup(body)                           → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
lw.auth.loginWithEmail({ email, password })    → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
lw.auth.loginWithGoogle({ id_token })          → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
lw.auth.logout()                               → Promise<void>
lw.auth.requestPasswordReset({ email })        → Promise<AuthPasswordResetResponse>
lw.auth.resetPassword({ email, reset_code, password }) → Promise<AuthPasswordResetResponse>
```

Tokens are stored automatically after every successful login.

### lw.checkout

```ts
lw.checkout.state(contentId: string)  → Promise<CheckoutStateResponse>
```

Use this to determine what the current visitor needs to do before accessing content:

```ts
const { checkout_state } = await lw.checkout.state('article-id')

switch (checkout_state.next_required_action) {
  case 'authenticate':
    // Show login/signup UI
    break
  case 'fund_wallet':
    // Show wallet funding UI (checkout_state.has_sufficient_funds === false)
    break
  case 'purchase':
    // Show purchase confirmation UI
    break
  case 'view_content':
    // User has access — fetch and render the content
    break
}
```

### lw.wallet

```ts
lw.wallet.balance()                                    → Promise<WalletBalanceResponse>
// { balance_cents: number }

lw.wallet.transactions(params?: PaginationParams)      → Promise<{ data: WalletTransactionItem[], pagination: PaginationMeta }>

lw.wallet.createPaymentSession({ amount_cents: number }) → Promise<WalletPaymentSessionResponse>
// { payment_url: string, session_id: string, ... } — redirect user to payment_url

lw.wallet.getPaymentStatus(sessionId: string)          → Promise<WalletPaymentStatusResponse>
// Poll after return from payment redirect to confirm funding
```

### lw.purchases

```ts
lw.purchases.create({ content_id: string })           → Promise<PurchaseResponse>
lw.purchases.list(params?: PaginationParams)          → Promise<{ data: PurchaseResponse[], pagination: PaginationMeta }>
lw.purchases.get(purchaseId: string)                  → Promise<PurchaseResponse>
```

### lw.content

```ts
lw.content.getWithAccess(contentId: string) → Promise<ContentWithAccessResponse>
// Returns content payload + access info. Requires a valid buyer session.
// content_type: 'markdown' | 'external_ref'
// content_body: plain text markdown (SDK decodes base64 automatically)
// content_uri: URL for external_ref content (Vimeo, YouTube, PDF, etc.)
```

### lw.seller

Operations scoped to the authenticated seller's store.
Store identity is derived server-side from the token — no `store_id` needed.

```ts
// Authenticate first (key-only → view; key + secret → full)
lw.seller.loginWithApiKey({ key, secret? })           → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
// key-only → view permission (read-only, sufficient for lw.seller.content.*)
// key + secret → full permission (read/write)

// 1. Obtain a view token (key-only is sufficient)
await lw.seller.loginWithApiKey({ key: 'your_api_key' })

// 2. List all store content
lw.seller.content.list()                              → Promise<ContentResponse[]>

// 3. Search by title, URI, or metadata (AND logic — at least one field required)
lw.seller.content.search({ title?: string, uri?: string, metadata?: Record<string, unknown> })
                                                      → Promise<ContentResponse[]>

// 4. Fetch a single item
lw.seller.content.get(contentId: string)              → Promise<ContentResponse>
```

## Full checkout flow example

```ts
const lw = Ledewire.init({ apiKey: 'your_api_key' })

const { checkout_state } = await lw.checkout.state('article-123')

switch (checkout_state.next_required_action) {
  case 'authenticate':
    await lw.auth.loginWithEmail({ email, password })
    break

  case 'fund_wallet':
    const session = await lw.wallet.createPaymentSession({ amount_cents: 500 })
    window.location.href = session.payment_url  // Stripe checkout
    break

  case 'purchase':
    await lw.purchases.create({ content_id: 'article-123' })
    break

  case 'view_content':
    const { content_type, content_body, content_uri } =
      await lw.content.getWithAccess('article-123')
    if (content_type === 'markdown') {
      renderMarkdown(content_body)  // plain text — SDK decodes base64 automatically
    } else {
      window.location.href = content_uri  // redirect to gated video/PDF
    }
    break
}
```

## Google OAuth sign-in setup

```ts
// Fetch the platform's Google Client ID before the user signs in:
const { google_client_id } = await lw.config.getPublic()
google.accounts.id.initialize({
  client_id: google_client_id,
  callback: async ({ credential }) => {
    await lw.auth.loginWithGoogle({ id_token: credential })
  },
})
google.accounts.id.renderButton(document.getElementById('signin-btn'), { theme: 'outline' })
```

## Key types

```ts
interface CheckoutStateResponse {
  content_id: string
  content_title: string
  price_cents: number
  checkout_state: {
    is_authenticated: boolean
    has_sufficient_funds: boolean | null  // null when not authenticated
    has_purchased: boolean
    next_required_action: 'authenticate' | 'fund_wallet' | 'purchase' | 'view_content'
  }
}

interface StoredTokens {
  accessToken: string
  refreshToken: string
  expiresAt: number  // Unix timestamp (ms)
}

interface TokenStorage {
  getTokens(): Promise<StoredTokens | null>
  setTokens(tokens: StoredTokens): Promise<void>
  clearTokens(): Promise<void>
}
```

## Error classes

All errors extend `LedewireError`. Import named subclasses for actionable handling:

```ts
import { LedewireError, AuthError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, PurchaseError } from '@ledewire/browser'

class LedewireError extends Error {
  statusCode: number        // HTTP status code
  code: number | undefined  // machine-readable code from API body
  message: string           // human-readable description from API body
}

class AuthError extends LedewireError {}       // 401 — bad credentials / session expired
class ForbiddenError extends LedewireError {}  // 403 — authenticated but access denied
class NotFoundError extends LedewireError {}   // 404 — resource not found
class PurchaseError extends LedewireError {}   // 409/422 — purchase validation failure
```

```ts
try {
  await lw.purchases.create({ content_id: 'article-123' })
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof AuthError) {
    showLoginModal()
  } else if (err instanceof PurchaseError) {
    showError('Purchase failed: ' + err.message)
  }
}
```
